title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect Patriots to acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 due to salary cap reasons.
The Patriots have shown interest in Brown, a 28-year-old Pro Bowler, despite high trade demands for draft picks.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasized strengthening the roster, hinting at potential moves to enhance the WR position.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Amanda Seyfried stars in 'The Testament of Ann Lee,' streaming now on Hulu, depicting the Quaker founder of the Shakers.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' directed by Nia DaCosta, is available on Netflix, continuing the zombie virus saga.
Ken Burns' documentary on Henry David Thoreau features Don Henley and Jeff Goldblum, streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 in their home opener, ending a five-game losing streak at Fenway Park.
Caleb Durbin broke an 0-for-19 slump with an RBI single, contributing to Boston's victory.
Pitcher Johan Oviedo was placed on the 15-day injured list due to a pitching elbow strain.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots start phase one of voluntary offseason workouts on April 20, focused on conditioning, meetings, and rehab.
Phase two begins with on-field drills the next three weeks, allowing walkthroughs but not full contact.
OTAs run on specific dates in late May and early June followed by a mandatory minicamp from June 15-17.

title: Patriots double up on edge rushers in latest ESPN mock draft
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots are projected to select edge rushers in the first and second rounds of the latest ESPN mock draft.
Cashius Howell from Texas A&M is predicted to be the Patriots' first-round pick; Gabe Jacas from Illinois in the second.
The Patriots face various team needs, including offensive tackle, despite focusing on edge rushers in this mock draft.

title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox secured a 5-2 win over the San Diego Padres at their home opener at Fenway Park.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, and Willson Contreras hit a pivotal home run, boosting team morale.
Marcelo Mayer and Caleb Durbin contributed key hits, signalling a potential turnaround for the struggling Red Sox.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover plans to start a body-worn camera program for police after a shooting incident without cameras drew attention.
Town Manager Melissa Rodrigues stated they will seek grants to reduce taxpayer costs for implementing the camera program.
Ex-officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons was acquitted of charges related to the incident, highlighting the lack of body cameras.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park as a San Diego Padre, receiving a warm welcome from Red Sox fans.
Bogaerts discussed the pressure of playing in Boston and compared it to expectations in San Diego.
Bogaerts highlighted Roman Anthony as a key future player for the Red Sox, noting his recent contract extension.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail connects 16 historic sites along a 2.5-mile path, attracting over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England introduces Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch coaster debuting in April for pass holders and open to the public April 17.
Sand Beach in Acadia National Park, Maine, was named among America's best beaches by Conde Nast Traveler.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and husband John McCauley filed a noise complaint against neighbors in Warwick, Rhode Island in 2024.
The couple won an injunction limiting noise to 60 decibels, but neighbors Avagyan and Beausoleil are appealing the decision.
Rhode Island Supreme Court is reviewing the injunction challenge, while neighbors dispute the noise and zoning violation claims.
